cited_spans.span_text: SIGMA DETECTION level=medium rule=Failed Logon Attempt tags=credential_access,t1110 event_id=4625 channel=Security TargetUserName=ADMINISTRATOR SubjectUserName=- IpAddress=136.144.176.69 WorkstationName=- LogonType=3

SIGMA DETECTION level=medium rule=Failed Logon Attempt tags=credential_access,t1110 event_id=4625 channel=Security TargetUserName=administrator SubjectUserName=- IpAddress=213.202.233.90 WorkstationName=Remmina LogonType=3

interpretations.justification:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The cited Sigma records explicitly identify Security event_id=4625 failed logon detections tagged credential_access,t1110 against administrator variants, including source IP addresses and LogonType=3. The detections are samples from a very large set of medium detections, so they establish hostile authentication pressure but not successful access on their own.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.text:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The host experienced credential-guessing or password-spray style failed network logon attempts against administrator-named accounts from external IP addresses; this anchors Q4 as attempted network logon access but does not by itself prove compromise.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
Confidence would decrease if the underlying Security records contradicted the Sigma extraction, or increase in scope if raw successful logons from the same IPs or immediately following these failures were found.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]

text: Sigma detection samples show failed Security logon attempts for ADMINISTRATOR from 136.144.176.69 and for administrator from 213.202.233.90, both with event_id=4625 and LogonType=3.

SIGMA DETECTION level=medium rule=RDP Interactive Logon tags=lateral_movement,t1021.001 event_id=4624 channel=Security TargetUserName=fred.rocba@outlook.com SubjectUserName=SRL-FORGE$ IpAddress=52.249.198.56 WorkstationName=SRL-FORGE LogonType=10

interpretations.justification:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The cited records explicitly show successful Security event 4624 logons for local account names srl-h and fredr with network LogonType=3 and source IPs. The Sigma record explicitly flags an RDP Interactive Logon for fred.rocba@outlook.com with LogonType=10 from the same 52.249.198.56 address seen for fredr network logons. This directly answers which accounts were accessed and how access occurred at the logon layer, though it does not prove whether the user was authorized.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.text:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
Accounts srl-h, fredr, and fred.rocba@outlook.com were accessed via successful network/RDP logons; the notable access path was NTLM network logon and RDP interactive logon from external IP addresses 174.196.200.9 and 52.249.198.56.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
Confidence would decrease if the Sigma RDP detection were not corroborated by the underlying Security record; confidence about authorization would require identity/provider or VPN records tying those IP addresses to legitimate users.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]

text: Security and Sigma records show successful logons on SRL-FORGE for srl-h, fredr, and fred.rocba@outlook.com: srl-h had EventID=4624 LogonType=3 from 174.196.200.9 on 2020-11-10, fredr had EventID=4624 LogonType=3 from 52.249.198.56 with WorkstationName=cobra on 2020-11-14, and Sigma flagged fred.rocba@outlook.com as an RDP Interactive Logon with LogonType=10 from 52.249.198.56 on 2020-11-14.

cited_spans.span_text: LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST

interpretations.justification:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The cited LNK records explicitly identify Recent shortcut targets under fredr's profile for SRL project folders (Airwolf, Blue Thunder, Gunstar), an exported PST file, and staging-like folders named Files of interest and Files from SRL system. Several of these records are timestamped on 2020-11-14 in the search results, close to successful fredr logons, but the observation deliberately cites only target paths because those are the verbatim spans available in the records.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.text:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The data accessed included SRL project folders and an exported email PST, with evidence of staging or collection folders on F: and Google Drive/My Drive paths. This supports Q2, what data was taken or targeted, but LNK records show access/path references rather than proving the file contents were successfully exfiltrated.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
Confidence would decrease if the LNK records were shown to be stale or unrelated to the November logons; confidence that data was actually taken would require corroborating copy, archive, cloud-sync, or network-transfer records for the same paths.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]

text: Recent LNK records for fredr show access to SRL project and export data, including SRL-Projects - Airwolf, SRL-Projects - Blue Thunder, SRL-Projects - Gunstar, SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst, Files of interest, and Files from SRL system.

cited_spans.span_text: LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST

interpretations.justification:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The cited LNK records explicitly place SRL project and PST data under G:\My Drive and OneDrive paths, and the cited prefetch records show the Google Drive, OneDrive, and Dropbox client executables ran. This supports likely cloud-sync transfer destinations, especially for the G:\My Drive and OneDrive paths. Confidence is medium rather than high because the cited artifacts show paths and client execution, not an item-level cloud upload completion record for each file.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.text:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The likely transfer destinations were cloud-sync locations: G:\My Drive for SRL project and exported PST data, and C:\Users\fredr\OneDrive\Documents\Outlook Files for backup.pst; Google Drive, OneDrive, and Dropbox client execution was present around the activity window.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
Confidence would increase with provider logs, OneDrive/Google Drive sync logs showing upload completion, or SRUM network usage for the exact sync application at the exact file-access time; confidence would decrease if the cloud paths were mounted read-only or represented stale shortcuts unrelated to November 2020 activity.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]

text: Transfer-related artifacts show SRL data paths in G:\My Drive and an Outlook PST path in C:\Users\fredr\OneDrive\Documents\Outlook Files. A prefetch artifact shows execution of GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE. A prefetch artifact shows execution of ONEDRIVE.EXE. A prefetch artifact shows execution of DROPBOX.EXE.

cited_spans.span_text: SIGMA DETECTION level=medium rule=RDP Interactive Logon tags=lateral_movement,t1021.001 event_id=4624 channel=Security TargetUserName=fred.rocba@outlook.com SubjectUserName=SRL-FORGE$ IpAddress=52.249.198.56 WorkstationName=SRL-FORGE LogonType=10
LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST

interpretations.justification:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The cited records cover the incident sequence: account logons for srl-h and fredr, a Sigma RDP interactive logon for fred.rocba@outlook.com, LNK access to the exported PST under G:\My Drive, SDELETE.EXE execution, and cloud-sync client execution. The record timestamps available in the index place these events in the November 2020 activity window, particularly 2020-11-10 and 2020-11-14.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.text:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The activity timeline ties together successful account access, SRL/email data access, cloud-sync client execution, and cleanup-related SDELETE execution during the November 2020 incident window.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
Confidence would decrease if event timestamp normalization was incorrect or if the cited artifacts were shown to be unrelated to the same user session; confidence would increase with a complete sessionized timeline from all Security, Prefetch, LNK, and SRUM records.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]

text: The timeline includes successful access to srl-h from 174.196.200.9, successful access to fredr from 52.249.198.56, an RDP Interactive Logon for fred.rocba@outlook.com from 52.249.198.56, Recent LNK access to SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst, execution of SDELETE.EXE, execution of DROPBOX.EXE, and execution of GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE.

interpretations.justification:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
This revision removes the specific dates that were challenged and confines the observation to the verbatim cited fields: EventID=4624, account names, logon types, source IP addresses, WorkstationName where present, and the Sigma RDP Interactive Logon record. The records directly substantiate successful network logon for srl-h and fredr and an RDP interactive logon detection for fred.rocba@outlook.com.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.text:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The successful-access finding is supported without relying on uncited date text: srl-h and fredr had successful NTLM network logons, and fred.rocba@outlook.com had an RDP interactive logon detection from the cited source IPs.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
Confidence would decrease if the underlying Security event for the Sigma RDP detection contradicted the Sigma extraction; confidence about exact event dates in the finding would require citing timestamp-bearing records or including the index timestamps in a separately supported timeline observation.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]

text: Security and Sigma records show successful logons on SRL-FORGE for srl-h, fredr, and fred.rocba@outlook.com: srl-h had EventID=4624 LogonType=3 from 174.196.200.9; fredr had EventID=4624 LogonType=3 from 52.249.198.56 with WorkstationName=cobra; and Sigma flagged fred.rocba@outlook.com as an RDP Interactive Logon with LogonType=10 from 52.249.198.56.

cited_spans.span_text: LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST

interpretations.justification:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
This revised interpretation follows the critic guidance: the cited LNK records substantiate SRL/PST path references under G:\My Drive and OneDrive, and the cited prefetch records substantiate execution of GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE, ONEDRIVE.EXE, and DROPBOX.EXE. The evidence does not by itself prove completed upload/sync, that DROPBOX.EXE handled SRL data, or item-level cloud transfer completion, so the conclusion is limited to potential cloud-sync destinations and client execution.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.text:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
The transfer-destination evidence supports potential Google Drive/My Drive and OneDrive locations for SRL/PST data references and shows cloud-client execution, but it does not prove completed upload/sync or Dropbox involvement with SRL data.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
interpretations.what_would_change_this_confidence:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
Confidence would increase with Google Drive, OneDrive, or Dropbox sync logs/cache metadata showing upload completion for the cited SRL/PST paths; confidence would decrease if the LNK records were stale or the clients were shown to have been inactive for the relevant user session.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]

text: Transfer-related artifacts show SRL and PST file references in G:\My Drive and C:\Users\fredr\OneDrive\Documents\Outlook Files. A prefetch artifact shows execution of GOOGLEDRIVEFS.EXE. A prefetch artifact shows execution of ONEDRIVE.EXE. A prefetch artifact shows execution of DROPBOX.EXE. The cited records do not state that uploads or sync completed.

[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
text:

Investigation of case rocba found evidence of credential-guessing against administrator-named accounts, successful network/RDP access to SRL-FORGE accounts, Recent-file references to SRL project and email export data, cloud-sync path/client artifacts, and cleanup-tool execution. The transfer finding is limited: the cited records show cloud paths and client execution, but not completed upload/sync.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]

text:
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE BEGIN]
Key Questions addressed: Q1 accounts accessed: srl-h, fredr, and fred.rocba@outlook.com are supported by revised observation O-006. Q2 data taken/targeted: SRL project folders, staging-like folders, and SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst are supported by O-003. Q3 transfer destination: G:\My Drive and OneDrive path references and cloud-client executions are supported by revised observation O-007, but completed upload/sync is not proven. Q4 access method: failed credential attacks, NTLM network logons, and RDP interactive logon evidence are supported by O-001 and O-006. Q5 timing: the November 2020 incident sequence is supported by O-005, with record timestamps in the case index linking logon, data-path access, cloud-client execution, and SDELETE execution.
[UNTRUSTED EVIDENCE END]
